page: Amazon Web Services c8in.16xlarge is a compute-optimized instance belonging to the c8in family, powered by dedicated Intel processors. Built on the AWS Nitro System hypervisor, this instance features 64 virtual CPUs mapped to 32 physical cores with 2.0 threads per core. The x86_64 architecture processors operate at a clock speed of 3.9 GHz. It is equipped with 128.0 GB of system memory, yielding a compute-focused ratio of 2.0 GB of RAM per vCPU.

This instance does not include local NVMe storage or integrated GPU hardware, relying instead on network-attached storage options. It is heavily optimized for network performance, delivering a baseline network bandwidth of 100 Gbps. This high-throughput network interface makes it highly suitable for workloads requiring rapid data transfer across cluster nodes or external services.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the c8in.16xlarge provides a dense allocation of high-frequency compute resources and high network capacity without the added expense of local storage or specialized accelerators. This makes it a cost-effective choice for network-bound and compute-intensive workloads. Appropriate use cases include high-performance computing, network appliances, distributed analytics, batch processing, and compute-heavy application servers.
description: Amazon Web Services c8in.16xlarge is a compute-optimized, network-optimized instance featuring 64 vCPUs and 32 physical Intel cores operating at 3.9 GHz on an x86_64 architecture. Utilizing the AWS Nitro System, it provides 128.0 GB of system memory, translating to 2.0 GB per vCPU, and lacks local storage or GPU accelerators. The instance is characterized by its high-speed networking capabilities, offering a baseline network bandwidth of 100 Gbps. This hardware profile delivers high compute density and throughput, making it qualitatively cost-efficient for workloads that do not require local NVMe drives. The c8in.16xlarge is designed for network-intensive and compute-heavy workloads, such as distributed analytics, high-performance computing, batch processing, and network virtual appliances.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c8in.16xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 64 vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and 100 Gbps network bandwidth, designed for high-throughput and compute-heavy workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c8in.16xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 64 vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and a baseline network bandwidth of 100 Gbps.
tagline: A compute-dense, network-optimized virtual server featuring high-frequency processors and high-speed networking for demanding distributed workloads.

128.0 GB of system memory providing 2.0 GB per vCPU
